metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5200
metadata.comment:
Rationale: "PSD-95 (DLG4) interacts with and is ubiquitinated by the E3 ligase Mdm2. In response to NMDA receptor activation, PSD-95 is ubiquitinated and rapidly removed from synaptic sites by proteasome-dependent degradation. Mutations that block PSD-95 ubiquitination prevent NMDA-induced AMPA receptor endocytosis."

Figure 2: NMDA treatment causes ubiquitination of PSD-95 that depends on a PEST motif.

Figure 3: reverse-IP in synaptic plasma membrane extracts shows interaction between PSD-95 and Mdm2, a Ubiquitin E3 Ligase. Confocal imaging of cultured hippocampal neurons shows Mdm2 localized to synapses.

Figure 4: Mdm2 ubiquitinates PSD-95 and regulates its expression levels and this depends on the PSD-95 PEST motif.

Further, authors show that the proteasome regulates PSD-95 degradation and ubiquitination of PSD-95 via its PEST motif regulates endocytosis of AMPA receptors (Fig. 5, 6). This is a presumed consequence of Mdm2 ubiquitinating PSD-95.. Experimental description: M&M: "Dissociated Hippocampal Neuron Culture - All rats were housed either in the Brown University Animal Care Facility or Vollum Institute Animal Care Facility, and all procedures were approved by institutional animal care and use committees. The hippocampus was removed from P0-P2 rats"
